serving.natural_language_portion: One typical serving is approximately 250 g (one bowl), providing around 60 kcal, 7.8 g protein, 2.5 g fat, and 1.75 g carbohydrate, with 1110 mg sodium.

nutrient_source.title: Minced Pork Tofu Soup — HPB Singapore Food Composition Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values taken directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Minced Pork Tofu Soup (cr_id F06100022). All core macros and secondary nutrients are sourced from this record with confidence 0.95.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson et al.; analogous estimate based on tofu and clear broth soup category
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for minced pork tofu soup. GI estimated at approximately 32 (low) based on the University of Sydney GI Database entry for tofu (GI ~15) and clear broth soups with minimal carbohydrate content. The dish contains only 0.7 g carbohydrate per 100 g, making the glycemic load negligible. GL per 250 g serving = (32 × 1.75 g available carbohydrate) / 100 ≈ 0.6.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 444 mg per 100 g; a 250 g serving delivers approximately 1110 mg sodium, representing nearly 50% of the 2300 mg daily limit.
hypertension.guidance: Patients with hypertension should request reduced soy sauce and no added salt, consume a smaller portion (100–150 g), and avoid drinking the broth to limit sodium intake.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Very low carbohydrate content (0.7 g per 100 g) and high protein (3.1 g per 100 g) support stable postprandial blood glucose.
type2_diabetes.guidance: Minced pork tofu soup is suitable for Type 2 Diabetes management due to its negligible carbohydrate load and high protein content; avoid adding starchy thickeners or high-sugar sauces.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Elevated sodium (444 mg per 100 g) raises cardiovascular risk; saturated fat is low at approximately 0.3 g per 100 g, which is favourable.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit portion size to reduce sodium exposure and request preparation with minimal soy sauce; the low saturated fat content is otherwise cardioprotective.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Pork is a red meat containing arachidonic acid (pro-inflammatory omega-6); sodium at 444 mg per 100 g may worsen tissue oedema.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Patients with rheumatoid arthritis should consider substituting minced pork with minced chicken or fish to reduce arachidonic acid load, and request reduced seasoning to limit sodium-driven tissue swelling.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Minced Pork Tofu Soup (肉碎豆腐汤) is a light, clear Singaporean Chinese soup consisting of finely minced pork and soft silken or firm tofu cubes simmered in a seasoned broth typically flavoured with soy sauce, sesame oil, ginger, and spring onion. The soup has a delicate, savoury flavour with a smooth, tender texture from the tofu and a subtle meatiness from the pork. It is commonly served as a side dish or light main at hawker centres and home kitchens across Singapore. Nutritionally, the dish is very low in calories (24 kcal per 100 g) and carbohydrates (0.7 g per 100 g), making it suitable for low-carbohydrate and diabetic dietary patterns. Protein content is moderate at 3.1 g per 100 g, contributed by both pork and tofu. The primary clinical concern is its high sodium content (444 mg per 100 g), which makes it unsuitable for hypertension patients without modification. Calcium (15 mg per 100 g) and iron (0.6 mg per 100 g) are present in modest amounts. The dish is dairy-free and soy-containing (allergen). It is not suitable for vegetarians or vegans due to the pork content, and is not halal.

The source is an approved primary regional source for Singaporean dishes. GI is an analogous estimate (low confidence for GI component only) due to absence of a direct GI measurement for this specific dish.
